entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: The legume crop being evaluated for growth and yield response to population and spacing

entities.name: PNUTGRO
entities.description: A crop growth model used to predict phenological development, canopy growth, and yields of groundnut

entities.name: PLANT POPULATION
entities.description: The number of plants per unit area used as a variable to test crop response

entities.name: ROW SPACING
entities.description: The distance between rows of plants used as a variable to test crop response

entities.name: PATANCHERU
entities.description: The specific site where field experiments were conducted

entities.name: ANDHRA PRADESH
entities.description: The state in India where the experiments were located

entities.name: PHENOLOGICAL DEVELOPMENT
entities.description: The timing and progression of growth stages in the groundnut crop

entities.name: LIGHT INTERCEPTION
entities.description: The process of the crop canopy capturing light for photosynthesis

entities.name: CANOPY GROWTH
entities.description: The development and expansion of the crop's foliage

entities.name: DM PRODUCTION
entities.description: The total dry matter produced by the groundnut plant

entities.name: POD YIELD
entities.description: The weight of pods produced per unit area

entities.name: SEED YIELD
entities.description: The weight of seeds produced per unit area

entities.name: ROBUT 33-1
entities.description: A specific cultivar of groundnut used for model calibration

entities.name: IRRIGATION
entities.description: The application of water to the crop used as an experimental condition

entities.name: LAI
entities.description: Leaf Area Index, a measure of canopy density used for model validation

entities.name: VEGETATIVE STAGE
entities.description: The initial growth phase of the plant before reproductive development

entities.name: REPRODUCTIVE STAGE
entities.description: The phase of the plant involving flowering and pod development

entities.name: RAINY SEASON
entities.description: The seasonal timing during which field experiments were conducted
